questions.question: A unique advantage of group therapy compared to individual therapy is that it:

options.text: Provides complete privacy for discussing sensitive issues
Allows clients to discover that others share similar struggles, reducing isolation
Eliminates the need for a trained therapist to facilitate sessions
Guarantees faster recovery than individual therapy in all cases

questions.explanation: Group therapy provides universality -- the discovery that others share similar struggles -- which reduces feelings of isolation. Group members also benefit from observing others' coping strategies, practicing social skills, and giving and receiving peer feedback.

questions.question: Family therapy differs from individual therapy primarily because it:

options.text: Uses only behavioral techniques and avoids cognitive approaches
Treats the family as an interconnected system rather than focusing on one individual
Is only appropriate for families with children under age 12
Requires all family members to have the same diagnosis

questions.explanation: Family therapy views the family as a system in which each member's behavior affects all others. Rather than identifying one person as the 'problem,' family therapists work to change dysfunctional patterns of interaction and communication within the system.
